reference_answer: NAICS major group 72 (Accommodation and Food Services).
+35% (the combined bonus from §3.1 and §3.2 may not exceed 35% of the base grant).
2 FTE.
25 FTE.
$1,000 (no outstanding state or county tax liability greater than $1,000).
Yes. Burnside Café was incorporated in Borden County on 2024-03-15 and applied on 2026-04-12 — approximately 25 months of operating history, well above §2.1's 12-month threshold.
Yes. The stacked §3.1 + §3.2 bonus would be 25% + 15% = 40%; under §3.4 the combined bonus is capped at 35%, so the effective bonus rate is 35%.
No. Cale's Borden County incorporation date is 2025-08-22 and the application date is 2026-04-15 — approximately 8 months, below the §2.1 12-month threshold.

Yes. Cale relocated from Riggs County (adjacent) on 2025-08-22, before the §4.1 cutoff of 2025-12-31, and was continuously incorporated there since 2018-06-01. §4.1 substitutes the prior incorporation date and §2.1 is satisfied.

App-2026-019 is in violation of §2.5 with $4,200 in outstanding county tax liability. §4.3 cure depends on whether Cale entered a payment plan with the Tax Office on or before 2026-03-31; the applicant claims a 2026-03-28 plan but the Tax Office has not yet transmitted documentation. Final eligibility is pending Tax Office confirmation.
No. §4.2 requires the applicant's primary business to be a sole proprietorship in NAICS major group 72; Hendricks is in NAICS 812 (pet care services). §4.2 cannot waive the §2.4 floor for Hendricks.
Denied on §2.4 grounds (1 FTE, below the 2-FTE lower bound). No exception applies.

Yes. Marsh is a sole proprietor in NAICS 722410 (NAICS major group 72), and approximately 18.3 months of continuous operation (incorporation 2024-09-30, application 2026-04-10) satisfy the §4.2 18-month threshold. §4.2 activates and waives the §2.4 lower bound.
Yes. NAICS 722410 falls within NAICS major group 72, satisfying §3.1.
Yes. Kevin Marsh is veteran-owned per §6, qualifying for the §3.2 +15% credit.

Pellicano is in violation of §2.5 ($1,200 outstanding state tax liability, exceeds the $1,000 threshold). §4.3 cure is timing-unavailable: Pellicano entered a payment plan on 2026-04-08, twenty-three days after the §4.3 deadline of 2026-03-31.

Kintaro has 1 FTE (sole proprietor), failing the §2.4 lower bound of 2. §4.2 is unavailable because Kintaro has approximately 16.8 months of operation (incorporation 2024-12-01, application 2026-04-25), short of the §4.2 18-month threshold.
2 (App-2026-014 Burnside Café LLC and App-2026-027 Marsh Common Tavern).
3 (App-2026-021 Hendricks Pet Grooming, App-2026-038 Pellicano Furniture Repair LLC, and App-2026-042 Kintaro Sushi).
1 (App-2026-019 Cale Industrial Coatings, Inc.).
$67,500 ($33,750 × 2). Pending applications are not included; if App-2026-019 is later approved at base only it would add up to $25,000.

Whether Cale Industrial entered a payment plan with the Borden County Tax Office on or before 2026-03-31. The applicant claims a 2026-03-28 plan but the Tax Office has not transmitted documentation as of packet close. The §4.3 cure depends on this confirmation.
2026-03-31. §4.3 requires entry into the payment plan ≥30 days before the application deadline of 2026-04-30.
By 2026-05-29. The Program Office requested the record by formal letter dated 2026-05-04.
No. Hendricks is denied at §2.4 (base eligibility); §3 bonuses apply only to applicants who pass base eligibility, so no §3.1 or §3.2 bonus is awarded.
No. §4.2 applies only to sole proprietorships in NAICS major group 72; Pellicano is in NAICS 811 (furniture repair). §4.2 is not available regardless of operating history.
No. §4.1 covers applicants who relocated to Borden County from an adjacent county. Kintaro was incorporated in Borden County on 2024-12-01 with no relocation; §4.1 does not apply.
